wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Getting Started section focused on STRM-Mapping toolkit onboarding. Explain the project's purpose as a cross-platform AI toolkit for producing Set-Theory Relationship Mapping (STRM) outputs between cybersecurity frameworks, control catalogs, and regulations. Document the core rules for repository usage, including running from repo root, working directory structure, and artifact management. Provide step-by-step installation instructions, prerequisite requirements (Node.js 18+, AI assistant setup), and basic usage examples. Include quick start workflows for different AI assistants (Claude, Gemini, GitHub Copilot). Demonstrate fundamental mapping operations with practical examples like NIST CSF 2.0 to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 mapping. Address common beginner scenarios and provide troubleshooting guidance for initial setup issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Claude Code Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Claude Code Integration focused on setting up and configuring the STRM mapping skill for Anthropic's Claude AI assistant. Document the skill definition process, including the SKILL.md configuration file structure, parameter specifications, and execution context setup. Explain the local settings configuration in settings.local.json, including API key setup, model selection, and performance tuning options. Detail the installation process through Claude's skill marketplace, skill activation procedures, and troubleshooting common integration issues. Include practical examples of invoking STRM mappings through Claude, parameter passing, and result interpretation. Document best practices for optimizing performance, handling rate limits, and managing skill updates. Address platform-specific considerations for Claude Code and provide guidance for migrating between different Claude versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Framework-to-Framework Mappings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Framework-to-Framework Mappings focused on cross-framework alignment and interoperability. Explain the methodology for mapping between different cybersecurity frameworks such as NIST 800-53, ISO 27001/27002, CIS Controls, and FedRAMP. Document the step-by-step process for preparing framework inputs, executing mappings, and interpreting results. Include practical examples showing how to map between major frameworks with detailed walkthroughs of input preparation, execution parameters, and output analysis. Address common challenges in framework alignment including terminology differences, control categorization variations, and maturity level discrepancies. Provide guidance on selecting appropriate frameworks for specific use cases, handling framework updates and revisions, and maintaining mapping currency. Document best practices for framework governance, stakeholder alignment, and continuous improvement of cross-framework relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Individual Script Documentation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for individual CLI script documentation covering all 10 scripts in the scripts/bin/ directory. Document strm-validate-csv.mjs for CSV format validation, strm-compute-strength.mjs for mathematical strength calculations, strm-generate-filename.mjs for standardized filename creation, strm-gap-report.mjs for gap analysis generation, strm-init-mapping.mjs for initial mapping setup, strm-check-existing-mapping.mjs for duplicate detection, strm-extract-json.mjs for JSON extraction, strm-map-extracted.mjs for extracted data mapping, strm-build-header.mjs for CSV header generation, and strm-list-input-files.mjs for input file discovery. For each script, provide complete parameter specifications, input/output formats, exit codes, error conditions, and practical usage examples. Include code snippets demonstrating proper invocation patterns and integration with other scripts in the workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extension Installation and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Gemini CLI Extension Installation and Setup section. Document the complete installation process for the STRM Mapping Gemini extension, including prerequisite requirements (Node.js 18+, Gemini CLI), dependency installation using npm/yarn, and extension registration with Gemini CLI. Explain the package.json configuration, build process (TypeScript compilation), and distribution mechanisms. Detail the gemini-extension.json configuration file including server settings, capabilities, and MCP protocol specifications. Document the TypeScript configuration and build scripts. Include step-by-step installation walkthrough, verification procedures, and initial setup validation. Address common installation issues, permission requirements, and platform-specific considerations for Windows, macOS, and Linux environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mathematical Foundation and Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Mathematical Foundation and Algorithms section focused on the core STRM computational mathematics. Document the five STRM relationship types (equal, subset_of, superset_of, intersects_with, not_related) and their mathematical definitions. Explain the strength scoring algorithm including base scores, confidence adjustments, and rationale type modifications. Detail the relationship calculation logic, score normalization (1-10 scale), and validation formulas. Include mathematical proofs and examples showing how different combinations produce specific strength values. Document the computational complexity of relationship calculations and performance characteristics for large datasets. Provide insights into the theoretical foundations of set theory relationships and their application to cybersecurity framework mapping.

wiki_catalogs.name: JSON Schema Definitions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the JSON Schema Definitions section focused on STRM toolkit data validation schemas. Document the controls.schema.json structure including control identifiers, descriptions, implementation status, and metadata fields. Detail the mappings.schema.json specification covering STRM relationship types, confidence levels, strength scores, and validation rules. Explain the risks.schema.json and threats.schema.json schemas including risk categories, threat actors, impact assessments, and severity classifications. Document the catalog.schema.json for framework and control catalog definitions. Include field specifications, data types, validation constraints, and required/optional properties. Provide examples of valid JSON instances, common validation errors, and troubleshooting guidance. Address schema versioning, evolution patterns, and migration strategies for schema updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: NIST IR 8477 Methodology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the NIST IR 8477 Methodology section focused on the formal mathematical foundation of Set-Theory Relationship Mapping. Explain the historical context and development of NIST IR 8477 as a standardized approach to cybersecurity framework mapping. Detail the core principles of set theory application in cybersecurity contexts, including the formal definitions of focal document elements (FDE), reference document elements (RDE), and set-theory relationships (STR). Document the mathematical rigor that enables automated reasoning and transitivity derivation. Explain how STRM differs from informal mapping approaches and the benefits of formal semantics. Include the theoretical underpinnings of cross-framework alignment and provide examples of how the methodology supports quantitative analysis and comparison across different cybersecurity standards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Concepts and Methodology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Core Concepts and Methodology section focused on NIST IR 8477 Set-Theory Relationship Mapping fundamentals. Explain the mathematical foundation of STRM methodology, including set theory relationships (equal, subset_of, superset_of, intersects_with, not_related). Document the strength scoring system, confidence levels, and rationale classifications. Detail the 12-column STRM CSV structure with field definitions and validation requirements. Explain the relationship between frameworks, controls, regulations, and risk/threat libraries. Include practical examples of each relationship type with concrete mapping scenarios. Document the validation rules, quality assurance processes, and manual review workflows. Address the theoretical underpinnings of cross-framework alignment and provide guidance on interpreting STRM results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Google Gemini CLI Extension
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Google Gemini CLI Extension focused on the Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for Google's Gemini AI assistant. Document the extension installation process, including package.json configuration, MCP server setup, and command registration. Explain the TypeScript-based implementation in src/index.ts, including the six MCP tools for deterministic operations: /strm:init, /strm:map, /strm:validate, /strm:gap-analysis, and /strm:extract-json. Detail the command definitions in commands/strm/ directory, parameter specifications, and execution contexts. Include practical examples of using slash commands, handling responses, and integrating with the broader Gemini ecosystem. Document the extension.json configuration, security considerations, and performance optimization techniques. Address troubleshooting common extension issues, debugging MCP connections, and maintaining compatibility across Gemini CLI versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Control-to-Control Alignments
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Control-to-Control Alignments focused on direct control mapping and catalog harmonization. Explain the process of aligning control catalogs from different sources to identify equivalent, overlapping, or complementary controls. Document the methodology for extracting control data, preparing control catalogs, and executing control-to-control mappings. Include comprehensive examples showing how to map controls between major catalogs like NIST 800-53, ISO 27001/27002, and industry-specific controls. Address control categorization schemes, control descriptions, implementation guidelines, and assessment criteria. Provide guidance on handling control variations, missing controls, and control updates. Document quality assurance techniques for control mapping validation, confidence scoring, and relationship strength calculations. Include practical examples of control catalog harmonization for compliance consolidation and operational efficiency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Batch Processing Workflows and Pipelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for batch processing workflows and automated pipelines focused on orchestrating multiple STRM scripts for large-scale operations. Document end-to-end workflow patterns including initial setup (strm-init-mapping), data extraction (strm-extract-json), validation (strm-validate-csv), strength computation (strm-compute-strength), gap analysis (strm-gap-report), and output generation (strm-generate-filename). Explain pipeline construction techniques, dependency management between scripts, error handling strategies, and conditional processing logic. Include practical examples of automated workflows for different mapping scenarios such as framework-to-framework crosswalks, control catalog alignments, and regulatory compliance mappings. Document performance optimization techniques, parallel processing strategies, and resource management for handling large datasets efficiently.

wiki_catalogs.name: MCP Tools Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive MCP Tools Reference documentation for the Gemini CLI extension's Model Context Protocol implementation. Document each of the six MCP tools: /strm:init for initialization operations, /strm:map for STRM mapping execution, /strm:gap-analysis for gap analysis generation, /strm:validate for CSV validation, and any additional tools. For each tool, detail the input parameters, expected data formats, processing logic, and output specifications. Explain the TOML command definitions, parameter validation using Zod schemas, and error handling mechanisms. Document the tool registration process, capability declarations, and integration with the core STRM processing engine. Include practical examples of tool invocation, parameter combinations, and result interpretation. Address tool-specific configurations, performance characteristics, and troubleshooting common issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSV Processing and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for CSV Processing and Validation focused on the data transformation and quality assurance systems. Document the custom CSV parser implementation including quote handling, escape sequence processing, and delimiter parsing. Explain the header normalization system, column indexing mechanism, and data validation workflows. Detail the STRM CSV schema validation including required fields, data type checks, and relationship consistency verification. Document the automated validation rules, error detection mechanisms, and warning systems. Include examples of valid/invalid CSV structures and their corresponding error messages. Address performance considerations for large CSV files and memory optimization techniques. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common CSV processing issues and encoding problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Risk and Threat Libraries
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Risk and Threat Libraries section focused on STRM toolkit enrichment data sources. Document the risks.json library structure including risk categories, risk types, likelihood assessments, impact matrices, and severity classifications. Explain the threats.json library covering threat actors, attack vectors, motivation factors, and capability levels. Detail the enrichment capabilities that integrate risk and threat data into STRM mappings, including automatic enrichment workflows and manual review processes. Document the relationship between risk/threat libraries and control catalogs, showing how contextual data enhances mapping accuracy. Include examples of enriched mappings, data transformation patterns, and integration workflows. Address library maintenance, update procedures, and version management. Provide guidance on extending libraries with custom risk/threat categories and maintaining data consistency across large deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: STRM Relationship Types and Scoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for STRM Relationship Types and Scoring focused on the five formal relationship categories defined by NIST IR 8477. Explain each relationship type (equal, subset_of, superset_of, intersects_with, not_related) with clear mathematical definitions and practical examples from cybersecurity frameworks. Document the strength scoring system from 1-10, including base scores for each relationship type and adjustments for confidence levels and rationale types. Detail the transitivity rules that allow derivation of relationships between mapped elements. Explain inverse relations and their applications. Provide concrete mapping scenarios showing how to apply each relationship type in practice. Include guidance on interpreting strength scores and making mapping decisions when relationships are ambiguous or require additional analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Assistant Platform Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AI Assistant Platform Integration section focused on multi-platform STRM toolkit deployment. Document the Agent Skills standard implementation for universal compatibility across AI assistants. Detail Claude Code skill configuration, Google Gemini CLI extension setup, and GitHub Copilot integration. Explain platform-specific installation procedures, skill definitions, and configuration requirements. Include setup instructions for OpenAI Codex, Cursor AI, Qoder, and other supported platforms. Document the skill manifest files, parameter specifications, and execution contexts. Address platform compatibility issues, troubleshooting common integration problems, and optimizing performance across different AI assistants. Provide migration guidance between platforms and best practices for consistent user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: GitHub Copilot Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for GitHub Copilot Setup focused on configuring the STRM mapping skill for GitHub's AI-powered coding assistant. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Regulation-to-Control Mappings focused on regulatory compliance alignment and regulatory-driven control identification. wiki_catalogs.name: Automation Integration and CI/CD

wiki_overview.content:
Based on my analysis of the STRM-Mapping project, I can now generate a comprehensive overview:

<blog>

# STRM-Mapping: NIST IR 8477 Set-Theory Relationship Mapping Toolkit

## Project Introduction

The STRM-Mapping project is a comprehensive multi-platform AI assistant skill and knowledge base designed to produce **Set-Theory Relationship Mapping (STRM)** CSV files between any two cybersecurity frameworks, control catalogs, or regulatory requirements. This toolkit implements the **NIST Interagency Report 8477** methodology, providing a standardized approach to crosswalk, align, and gap-analyze cybersecurity documentation.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Standardization**: Provide a deterministic, mathematically precise methodology for mapping between cybersecurity frameworks
- **Automation**: Enable AI assistants to automatically generate STRM CSV files with minimal human intervention
- **Flexibility**: Support mapping across diverse document types including frameworks, regulations, policies, and control catalogs
- **Quality Assurance**: Implement validation rules and manual review processes to ensure mapping accuracy

### Target Audience

- **Security Professionals**: Risk managers, compliance officers, and security architects
- **AI Assistant Users**: Professionals leveraging AI coding assistants for security documentation
- **Organizations**: Entities requiring framework alignment, gap analysis, or compliance mapping
- **Research Institutions**: Academic and government entities studying cybersecurity framework relationships

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular, multi-platform architecture designed for cross-assistant compatibility:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Repository Root] --> B[Skills Layer]
    A --> C[Knowledge Base]
    A --> D[Scripts Engine]
    A --> E[Working Directory]
    
    B --> B1[Agent Skills]
    B --> B2[Claude Code Skill]
    B --> B3[Gemini CLI Extension]
    B --> B4[Copilot Instructions]
    
    C --> C1[Methodology Reference]
    C --> C2[Schema Definitions]
    C --> C3[Risk/Threat Libraries]
    
    D --> D1[Core Functions]
    D --> D2[Validation Engine]
    D --> D3[File Processing]
    
    E --> E1[Input Files]
    E --> E2[Output Artifacts]
    E --> E3[Scratch Files]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several interconnected layers:

**Skill Layer**: Platform-specific implementations that integrate with AI assistants through the Agent Skills standard
**Knowledge Layer**: Structured data definitions and methodology references
**Processing Layer**: Core algorithms for mapping computation and validation
**Storage Layer**: Working directory structure for input/output management

### Design Patterns

- **Strategy Pattern**: Different mapping strategies for various document types
- **Template Method**: Standardized workflow across all mapping operations
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic creation of mapping artifacts based on input types
- **Observer Pattern**: Validation and quality assurance throughout the process

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Primary Skill Definition**: `skills/strm-mapping/SKILL.md`
- Defines the core STRM mapping methodology
- Specifies AI assistant integration points
- Contains detailed workflow instructions

**Core Processing Library**: `scripts/lib/strm-core.mjs`
- Implements mathematical relationship calculations
- Provides CSV parsing and generation utilities
- Contains validation and quality assurance functions

### Core Modules

**Mathematical Foundation**: The strength scoring system implements NIST IR 8477 methodology:
- Base scores: equal (10), subset/superset (7), intersects (4), not_related (0)
- Confidence adjustments: high (+0), medium (-1), low (-2)
- Rationale adjustments: semantic/functional (+0), syntactic (-1)

**File Processing Engine**: Handles multiple input formats:
- CSV, JSON, YAML, TOML, PDF, and Markdown
- Automatic extraction and normalization
- Cross-platform compatibility

### Configuration Approach

**Multi-Platform Support**: The project maintains compatibility across:
- Claude Code (manual installation required)
- OpenAI Codex (auto-discovered)
- Google Gemini CLI (skill + extension)
- GitHub Copilot (context injection)
- Cursor AI (auto-discovered)
- Qoder (auto-discovered)
- Aider (explicit activation)

**Working Directory Management**: All processing occurs within `working-directory/` to prevent contamination of the repository root.

### External Dependencies

**Gemini Extension**: `gemini-extension/package.json`
- Node.js SDK for Model Context Protocol
- TypeScript compilation support
- Cross-platform build automation

**Core Dependencies**: Minimal external requirements:
- Node.js 18+ for script execution
- Standard Node.js modules (path, fs, readline)
- JSON Schema validation for data integrity

### Integration Points

**Agent Skills Standard**: Universal compatibility through the Agent Skills open standard
**Platform-Specific Integrations**: Tailored implementations for each AI assistant
**File System Integration**: Direct access to repository files and working directory

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[User Request] --> B[Skill Activation]
    B --> C[Input Processing]
    C --> D[Mapping Engine]
    D --> E[Validation Layer]
    E --> F[Output Generation]
    
    G[Knowledge Base] --> D
    H[Schema Definitions] --> E
    I[Working Directory] --> C
    I --> F
    
    J[Platform Adapter] --> B
    K[CLI Tools] --> D
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Comprehensive Mapping Types**:
- Framework-to-Framework crosswalks
- Framework-to-Control mappings
- Control-to-Control alignments
- Regulation-to-Control mappings
- Policy-to-Framework mappings
- Risk-to-Control enrichment
- Threat-to-Risk mappings

**Advanced Validation System**:
- Automated CSV structure validation
- Mathematical strength score verification
- Relationship consistency checking
- Manual review workflow integration

### Implementation Highlights

**Template System**: `TEMPLATE_Set Theory Relationship Mapping (STRM).csv`
- Standardized 12-column CSV structure
- Target-specific column headers
- Comprehensive metadata fields

**Quality Assurance Pipeline**: 
- Pre-mapping duplicate detection
- Post-processing validation
- Gap analysis generation
- Manual review logging

**Risk and Threat Enrichment**: `knowledge/library/`
- SCF 2025.4 risk catalog integration
- Threat library for comprehensive mapping
- Materiality-based relationship derivation

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> InputCollection
    InputCollection --> Processing
    Processing --> Validation
    Validation --> QualityAssurance
    QualityAssurance --> OutputGeneration
    OutputGeneration --> [*]
    
    Processing --> RiskEnrichment
    RiskEnrichment --> Validation
    
    Validation --> Correction: Issues Found
    Correction --> QualityAssurance: Resolved
```

The STRM-Mapping toolkit represents a sophisticated approach to cybersecurity framework alignment, combining mathematical rigor with practical AI-assisted processing to deliver reliable, reproducible mapping results across diverse organizational needs.

---

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [SKILL.md](skills/strm-mapping/SKILL.md)
- [strm-core.mjs](scripts/lib/strm-core.mjs)
- [controls.schema.json](knowledge/controls.schema.json)
- [package.json](gemini-extension/package.json)
- [example-framework-to-control.md](examples/example-framework-to-control.md)
- [TEMPLATE.csv](TEMPLATE_Set Theory Relationship Mapping (STRM).csv)

</blog>
